channel.title: NHC Atlantic
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Atlantic, Caribbean, and the Gulf of America

item.title: Atlantic Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABNT20 KNHC 231742
TWOAT

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
200 PM EDT Thu Jul 23 2026

For the North Atlantic...Caribbean Sea and the Gulf of America:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Bertha, located inland near the Louisiana/Texas border.

Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days.

$$
Forecaster D. Zelinsky
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Bertha (AT2/AL022026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...BERTHA INLAND NEAR THE TEXAS-LOUISIANA BORDER... ...HEAVY RAINS SPREADING ACROSS PORTIONS OF COASTAL TEXAS... As of 1:00 PM CDT Thu Jul 23 the center of Bertha was located near 29.9, -93.8 with movement W at 10 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1004 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 45 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Bertha
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...BERTHA INLAND NEAR THE TEXAS-LOUISIANA BORDER... ...HEAVY RAINS SPREADING ACROSS PORTIONS OF COASTAL TEXAS...

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Public Advisory Number 17a
item.description:
Issued at 100 PM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026 
000
WTNT32 KNHC 231740
TCPAT2
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Bertha Intermediate Advisory Number 17A
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
Issued by the NWS Weather Prediction Center College Park MD
100 PM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026
 
...BERTHA INLAND NEAR THE TEXAS-LOUISIANA BORDER...
...HEAVY RAINS SPREADING ACROSS PORTIONS OF COASTAL TEXAS...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 100 PM CDT...1800 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...29.9N 93.8W
ABOUT 40 MI...60 KM WSW OF LAKE CHARLES LOUISIANA
ABOUT 75 MI...115 KM NE OF GALVESTON TEXAS
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...45 MPH...75 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...W OR 275 DEGREES AT 10 MPH...17 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1004 MB...29.65 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
CHANGES WITH THIS ADVISORY:
 
None.
 
SUMMARY OF WATCHES AND WARNINGS IN EFFECT:
 
A Tropical Storm Warning is in effect for...
* The Gulf Coast from Morgan City, Louisiana, to Sargent, Texas
 
A Tropical Storm Warning means that tropical storm conditions are
expected somewhere within the warning area, in this case within 24
hours.
 
For storm information specific to your area, including possible
inland watches and warnings, please monitor products issued by your
local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 100 PM CDT (1800 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Bertha was
located near latitude 29.9 North, longitude 93.8 West. Bertha is
moving toward the west near 10 mph (17 km/h), and this motion with 
some increase in forward speed is expected during the next day or 
so. On the forecast track, the center of Bertha will move across 
portions of eastern Texas during the next several hours.

Maximum sustained winds remain near 45 mph (75 km/h) with higher
gusts. Bertha is expected to gradually weaken, and it is forecast 
to dissipate in a day or so.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 160 miles (260 km)
south of the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 1004 mb (29.65 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
Key messages for Tropical Storm Bertha can be found in the Tropical
Cyclone Discussion under AWIPS header MIATCDAT2 and WMO header
WTNT42 KNHC.
 
WIND:  Tropical storm conditions will continue spreading across 
portions of the Louisiana and Texas coasts within the warning area 
into the evening.
 
STORM SURGE:  The combination of storm surge and the tide will
cause normally dry areas near the immediate coast to be flooded by
rising waters moving inland from the shoreline. The water could
reach the following heights above ground somewhere in the indicated
areas if the peak surge occurs at the time of high tide...
 
Port Bolivar, TX to Port Fourchon, LA...1-2 ft
 
The deepest water will occur along the immediate coast.
Surge-related flooding depends on the relative timing of the surge
and the tidal cycle, and can vary greatly over short distances. For
information specific to your area, please see products issued by
your local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
For a complete depiction of areas at risk of storm surge inundation,
please see the National Weather Service Peak Storm Surge Graphic,
available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?peakSurge.
 
RAINFALL: Bertha is expected to produce total rainfall
amounts of 2 to 4 inches with isolated totals to 6 inches through
Thursday night along Texas Gulf Coast and South Texas. This rainfall
may produce isolated flash flooding, especially in urban areas.
 
For a complete depiction of forecast rainfall associated with
Bertha, please see the National Weather Service Storm Total Rainfall
Graphic available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?rainqpf and
the Flash Flood Risk graphic at
hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ero.
 
SURF: Swells generated by Bertha will continue spreading westward
along the northern and northwestern Gulf Coast. These swells are
likely to cause life-threatening surf and rip current conditions.
Please consult products from your local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 400 PM CDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Cangialosi/R. Zelinsky
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Advisory Number 17
item.description:
Issued at 1500 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
WTNT22 KNHC 231436
TCMAT2
 
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  17
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026
ISSUED BY THE NWS WEATHER PREDICTION CENTER COLLEGE PARK MD
1500 UTC THU JUL 23 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.6N  93.2W AT 23/1500Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST OR 275 DEGREES AT   9 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1004 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  40 KT WITH GUSTS TO  50 KT.
34 KT....... 20NE 140SE 140SW   0NW.
4 M SEAS....  0NE 150SE   0SW   0NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.6N  93.2W AT 23/1500Z
AT 23/1200Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 29.5N  92.6W
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0000Z 29.7N  95.2W...INLAND
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT...  0NE 110SE  90SW   0NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1200Z 29.3N  98.5W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  20 KT...GUSTS  30 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/0000Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 29.6N  93.2W
 
INTERMEDIATE PUBLIC ADVISORY...WTNT32 KNHC/MIATCPAT2...AT 23/1800Z
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 23/2100Z
 
$$
FORECASTER CANGIALOSI
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Discussion Number 17
item.description:
Issued at 1000 AM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026 
000
WTNT42 KNHC 231441
TCDAT2
 
Tropical Storm Bertha Discussion Number  17
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
Issued by the NWS Weather Prediction Center College Park MD
1000 AM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026

The center of Bertha has been moving along or just offshore of
southwestern Louisiana coast during the past few hours.  Data from
the Air Force Reserve reconnaissance aircraft suggest that the
circulation is becoming increasingly elongated, likely due to the
storm's persistent convective asymmetry.  In fact, infrared
satellite and radar images show almost all of the convection is
located south of the center, and there is large region of heavy rain
and embedded thunderstorms approaching the Texas coast. The initial
intensity is held at 40 kt, but these winds are occurring offshore
over the southern half of the circulation.
 
The storm has been moving erratically over the past 12 hours or so,
but smoothing through the wobbles and potential center reformations
yields a westward motion at 9 kt.  Bertha is expected to move a
little faster to the west, on the south side of a mid-level ridge,
and that should bring the storm inland over Texas late this
afternoon or evening.  Due to the poor structure of
Bertha and expected continued northerly vertical wind shear, little
change is intensity is anticipated before the storm makes its final
landfall in Texas.  Bertha will likely become a weak remnant low
or dissipate entirely in about 24 hours.
 
 
Key Messages:
 
1. Tropical storm conditions are expected to spread westward along
the northwestern Gulf Coast from south central Louisiana to the
upper Texas coast today.
 
2. A Coastal Flood Warning remains in effect for portions of the
Mississippi and Louisiana coastlines, where moderate coastal
flooding continues.
 
3. Isolated flash flooding is possible along the Texas Gulf Coast
and South Texas through tonight.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  23/1500Z 29.6N  93.2W   40 KT  45 MPH
 12H  24/0000Z 29.7N  95.2W   35 KT  40 MPH...INLAND
 24H  24/1200Z 29.3N  98.5W   20 KT  25 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 36H  25/0000Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Cangialosi
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Wind Speed Probabilities Number 17
item.description:
Issued at 1500 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
FONT12 KNHC 231437
PWSAT2
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  17           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026               
ISSUED BY THE NWS WEATHER PREDICTION CENTER COLLEGE PARK MD         
1500 UTC THU JUL 23 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 1500Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 29.6 NORTH...LONGITUDE 93.2 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED    
WINDS NEAR 40 KTS...45 MPH...75 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   ATLANTIC STANDARD TIME (AST)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   EASTERN  DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   CENTRAL  DAYLIGHT TIME (CDT)...SUBTRACT 5 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        12Z THU AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       12Z THU 00Z FRI 12Z FRI 00Z SAT 12Z SAT 12Z SUN 12Z MON
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             00Z FRI 12Z FRI 00Z SAT 12Z SAT 12Z SUN 12Z MON 12Z TUE
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
GFMX 280N 910W 34 22   X(22)   X(22)   X(22)   X(22)   X(22)   X(22)
 
MORGAN CITY LA 34  3   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
GFMX 280N 930W 34 99   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)
 
LAKE CHARLES   34  4   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
CAMERON LA     34 58   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)
 
KOUNTZE TX     34  5   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
PORT ARTHUR TX 34 20   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)
 
GALVESTON TX   34 84   1(85)   X(85)   X(85)   X(85)   X(85)   X(85)
 
HOUSTON TX     34 34   1(35)   X(35)   X(35)   X(35)   X(35)   X(35)
 
SAN ANTONIO TX 34  X   5( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
FREEPORT TX    34 77   X(77)   X(77)   X(77)   X(77)   X(77)   X(77)
 
GFMX 280N 950W 34 19   X(19)   X(19)   X(19)   X(19)   X(19)   X(19)
 
HIGH ISLAND TX 34 80   X(80)   X(80)   X(80)   X(80)   X(80)   X(80)
 
MATAGORDA TX   34 33   5(38)   X(38)   X(38)   X(38)   X(38)   X(38)
 
PORT O CONNOR  34  9   8(17)   X(17)   X(17)   X(17)   X(17)   X(17)
 
ROCKPORT TX    34  X   4( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER CANGIALOSI
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
